_publications._publicationTitle: Optical dopamine monitoring with dLight1 reveals mesolimbic phenotypes in a mouse model of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: Spontaneous firing rates (right) were lower (unpaired t-test; t28 = 2.58, p=0.0w) in Nf1+/- putative dopaminergic neurons (n = 18) compared to Nf1+/+ neurons (n = 12)
details: Nf1+/- putative dopaminergic neurons (n=18) showed lower spontaneous firing rates compared to Nf1+/+ neurons (n=12) (unpaired t-test; t28=2.58, p<0.05)
